jobTitle: Field assistants (m/f/d) in New Caledonian Crow cognition and behaviour research project (CrowCoG)
jobDescription: The Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology unites scientists with various backgrounds (natural sciences and humanities) whose aim is to investigate the history of humankind from an interdisciplinary perspective with the help of comparative analyses of genes, cultures, cognitive abilities, languages and social systems of past and present human populations as well as those of primates closely related to human beings. The Department of Linguistic and Cultural Evolution aims to answer big picture questions about the evolution of language and culture. Our focus is on describing and explaining the major patterns of linguistic and cultural variation across the globe. We tackle these questions by developing novel language documentation methods, global linguistic and cultural databases, and analyses using evolutionary theories and computational methods. Our aviary-based research work primarily focuses on conducting cognitive and behavioural studies with temporarily-captive New Caledonian crows in our purpose-built aviary. Aviary-based assistants will help with our day-to-day captive work including but not limited to: caring for the crows (e.g., food preparation, health checks, aviary cleaning and maintenance); helping to prepare for and/or conduct experiments; experimental apparatus maintenance; and processing and entering data. Field-based research mainly focuses on conducting observational studies on New Caledonian crow foraging behaviour in the wild. This involves capturing and banding wild birds so that they can be identified, and collecting data on their natural foraging behavior through a combination of direct observation and remote camera-trapping. Field-based assistants will help with a wide range of tasks including but not limited to: trapping and banding crows; transporting, installing, and maintaining heavy field equipment; surveying sites and documenting wild crow behaviors; and processing and entering data (e.g., from video).
